question.text: An appeal to the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) against an order of SEBI or Adjudicating Officer under the Depositories Act must be filed within:

answer.text: 45 days from the date of receipt of the order
90 days from the date of receipt of the order
30 days from the date of receipt of the order
60 days from the date of receipt of the order

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Depositories Act, every appeal to the SAT shall be filed within a period of 45 days from the date on which a copy of the order made by SEBI or Adjudicating Officer is received by the aggrieved party. The SAT may entertain an appeal after 45 days if satisfied there was sufficient cause for the delay. **The Trap:** The 45-day appeal period to SAT is different from the 60-day appeal period to the Supreme Court against SAT orders. Candidates may confuse these two timelines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Timeline sequence: SEBI/AO order -> 45 days to appeal to SAT -> SAT order -> 60 days to appeal to Supreme Court. Both have provisions for condonation of delay.
